segments.speaker: Professor
segments.text: Let's go around and introduce ourselves briefly.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Hi everyone. I'm Jongkuk, and I'm from Korea.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I'm starting the PhD program in architecture this semester.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: My background is in architecture and design research, and I'm especially interested in housing, urban systems, and everyday life.

segments.speaker: Classmate
segments.text: When you say everyday life, what do you mean?

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I mean the ordinary routines that buildings quietly shape - how people cook, rest, commute, gather, or feel at home.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Thanks. I'm still new to Boston, so I'm also looking forward to learning from the city itself.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Only briefly. So if I look lost for the first few weeks, that's probably accurate.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Glad to hear it. I'm excited to work with everyone.
